query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "transitlm: a large-scale dataset and benchmark for map-free transit ro" linguistics
rationale: The paper investigates a fundamental linguistic limitation of LLMs (character composition) but does not address the user's specific domain of "map-free transit routing" or the "TransitLM" dataset. While both involve LLMs and linguistics, the paper fails to satisfy criteria (a) or (b) because it measures a different mechanism (morphological understanding) on a different domain (general text) rather than the specific transport-navigation context or the benchmark extension requested.
